summary: Typosquat of Anthropic SDK ships install-time multi-stage dropper, macOS TCC bypass, and crypto-wallet stealer
details: Package is published as `free-anthropic-claude` with a description claiming to be the 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper', but the bundled README is for an unrelated `cachesync-helper` library — the SDK identity is a lure. `package.json` declares `postinstall: node lib/cli.js`, which on install reaches a `_prefetch()` path in `lib/index.js` that POSTs to four hardcoded IPs (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146) on port 8443 with TLS verification disabled, base64-decodes the response into files under `~/.local/share/prometheus/`, and spawns `python` on the dropped `main.py` in a detached process. If no usable Python is found, the script bootstraps one — downloading Miniconda from `repo.anaconda.com` via curl/wget on Linux, running `winget install Python.Python.3.12` on Windows, or `brew install python3` on macOS — solely to execute the dropped payload. On macOS the postinstall writes directly into `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db` via `sqlite3`, inserting `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData` grants for Terminal.app, the node binary, and `/usr/bin/python3` to bypass Apple's privacy consent prompts and silently grant Full Disk Access to the dropped Python. Before executing the payload, the script pip-installs `bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `pycryptodome`, `psutil`, and `eth-account` with `--break-system-packages` — the standard toolkit for deriving keys from BIP-39 mnemonics and signing Ethereum transactions, indicating cryptocurrency wallet/seed theft. Sensitive APIs are split-string obfuscated throughout (`['htt','ps'].join('')`, `['child','_pro','cess']`, `['ba','se','64']`, `['exec','File','Sync']`, and `module[['constr','uctor'].join('')]['_l'+'oad']` to reach Node's internal module loader) to evade static scanners. Installing this package on a default `npm install` immediately compromises the host: remote code execution, OS-level privacy-consent bypass on macOS, and crypto-wallet exfiltration tooling staged for execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
